methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
General Matter, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Scott Nolan and Lee Robinson, has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation landscape includes 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers in the nuclear sector, including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient datapoints within narrow stage and valuation-tier filters—only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage comparable existed, below the threshold of 3 needed for robust cross-sectional analysis. Accordingly, the primary valuation method applied stage-progression analysis, using the $50M raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $100M.

The triangulated range is $85M (low) to $115M (high), with a median of $100M. This single-method result carries elevated uncertainty given reliance on one approach and the thinness of nuclear energy comparables at General Matter's stage. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 2.0x step multiplier to this sector's capital intensity and the degree to which public nuclear comps reflect private frontier valuations. Additional comparable data or revenue/unit-economics visibility would strengthen conviction in this range.
